# Phase 10.0.10 Uncertainty Survivability Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, autonomous future-adaptation systems, self-modifying survivability systems, future-governance authority, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve constitutional continuity, legitimacy, sovereignty, and fail-closed governance across unknown future conditions, black-swan events, technological shifts, institutional mutations, and long-horizon uncertainty.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional uncertainty-survivability doctrine.
- Future-risk resilience philosophy.
- Unknown-risk containment framework.
- Black-swan governance survivability standards.
- Long-horizon uncertainty continuity architecture.
- Civilization adaptability without instability doctrine.
- Constitutional future-proofing philosophy.
- Governance uncertainty resilience architecture.
- Technological-shift survivability doctrine.
- Institutional unknown-risk survivability standards.
- Phase 10.0.10 uncertainty survivability summary.

## Validation Checklist

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No uncertainty-governance layer creates runtime authority.
- No black-swan review bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No adaptability layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No uncertainty-survivability framework weakens auditability.

## Review Notes

- Uncertainty survivability remains human-reviewed, auditable, non-runtime, non-operational, and non-self-modifying.
- Adaptability preserves constitutional identity and does not create autonomous adaptation.
- Future-proofing preserves humility and fail-closed review rather than claiming complete prediction.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, autonomous future-adaptation systems, self-modifying survivability systems, or execution systems.
